channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: 6-Year Ransomware Campaign Targets Turkish Homes & SMBs
item.description: While enterprises breaches make more headlines, smaller incidents tend to be under-reported, if at all, allowing campaigns to last longer with less disruption....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Critical Nginx UI auth bypass flaw now actively exploited in the wild
item.description: A critical vulnerability in Nginx UI with Model Context Protocol (MCP) support is now being exploited in the wild for full server takeover without authentication. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New AgingFly malware used in attacks on Ukraine govt, hospitals
item.description: A new malware family named 'AgingFly' has been identified in attacks against local governments and hospitals that steal authentication data from Chromium-based browsers and WhatsApp messenger. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical MCP Integration Flaw Puts NGINX at Risk
item.description: Attackers can abuse the near-maximum severity flaw in nginx-ui to restart, create, modify, and delete NGINX configuration files....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: WordPress plugin suite hacked to push malware to thousands of sites
item.description: More than 30 WordPress plugins in the EssentialPlugin package have been compromised with malicious code that allows unauthorized access to websites running them. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Navigating the Unique Security Risks of Asia's Digital Supply Chain
item.description: Regulatory differences, interconnected digital ecosystems, and the rise of AI have created a complex supply chain Asian organizations must wrangle....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Signed software abused to deploy antivirus-killing scripts
item.description: A digitally signed adware tool has deployed payloads running with SYSTEM privileges that disabled antivirus protections on thousands of endpoints, some in the educational, utilities, government, and h...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: n8n Webhooks Abused Since October 2025 to Deliver Malware via Phishing Emails
item.description: Threat actors have been observed weaponizing n8n, a popular artificial intelligence (AI) workflow automation platform, to facilitate sophisticated phishing campaigns and deliver malicious payloads or ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft pays $2.3M for cloud and AI flaws at Zero Day Quest
item.description: Microsoft has awarded $2.3 million to security researchers after receiving nearly 700 submissions during this year's Zero Day Quest hacking contest. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Prepping for 'Q-Day': Why Quantum Risk Management Should Start Now
item.description: Quantum computers are coming and may impact systems in unexpected ways, and it will "take years to be fully quantum-safe, if ever," cryptography expert warns....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: CISA flags Windows Task Host vulnerability as exploited in attacks
item.description: CISA warned U.S. government agencies to secure their systems against a Windows Task Host privilege escalation vulnerability that could allow attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Audit: Big Tech Often Ignores CA Privacy Law Opt-Out Requests
item.description: Google, Meta, and Microsoft about half the time don't comply with requests to opt out of online tracking per a California law mandate, privacy watchdog finds....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Rolling Networks: Securing the Transportation Sector
item.description: Modern trucks are rolling networks packed with sensors, connectivity, and attack surfaces, creating new cyber risks. NMFTA's Cybersecurity Conference brings industry leaders together to tackle emergin...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Actively Exploited nginx-ui Flaw (CVE-2026-33032) Enables Full Nginx Server Takeover
item.description: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting nginx-ui, an open-source, web-based Nginx management tool, has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-20...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: April Patch Tuesday Fixes Critical Flaws Across SAP, Adobe, Microsoft, Fortinet, and More
item.description: A number of critical vulnerabilities impacting products from Adobe, Fortinet, Microsoft, and SAP have taken center stage in April's Patch Tuesday releases. Topping the list is an SQL injection vulnera...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft, Salesforce Patch AI Agent Data Leak Flaws
item.description: Two recently fixed prompt injections in Salesforce Agentforce and Microsoft Copilot would have enabled an external attacker to leak sensitive data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft: April updates trigger BitLocker key prompts on some servers
item.description: Microsoft confirmed on Tuesday that some Windows Server 2025 devices will boot into BitLocker recovery after installing the April 2026 KB5082063 Windows security update. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Deterministic + Agentic AI: The Architecture Exposure Validation Requires
item.description: Few technologies have moved from experimentation to boardroom mandate as quickly as AI. Across industries, leadership teams have embraced its broader potential, and boards, investors, and executives a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft fixes bug behind Windows Server 2025 automatic upgrades
item.description: Microsoft has finally fixed a known issue that was causing systems running Windows Server 2019 and 2022 to "unexpectedly" upgrade to Windows Server 2025. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Issues Patches for SharePoint Zero-Day and 168 Other New Vulnerabilities
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released updates to address a record 169 security flaws across its product portfolio, including one vulnerability that has been actively exploited in the wild. Of these 169 vulner...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.4-Cyber with Expanded Access for Security Teams
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday unveiled GPT-5.4-Cyber, a variant of its latest flagship model, GPT‑5.4, that's specifically optimized for defensive cybersecurity use cases, days after rival Anthropic unveiled its ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Bets $10B to Boost Japan's AI, Cybersecurity
item.description: The deal aims to accelerate AI adoption, train workers, and develop cybersecurity partnerships — the latest move by a hyperscaler to compete for sovereign AI and data centers....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft adds Windows protections for malicious Remote Desktop files
item.description: Microsoft has introduced new Windows protections to defend against phishing attacks that abuse Remote Desktop connection (.rdp) files, adding warnings and disabling risky shared resources by default. ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Crypto-exchange Kraken extorted by hackers after insider breach
item.description: The Kraken cryptocurrency exchange announced that a cybercrime group is trying to extort the company by threatening to release videos showing internal systems that host client data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly di...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
item.description: Elevation-of-privilege bugs accounted for more than half of the 165 vulnerabilities patched, with two zero-days in that mix....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Over 100 Chrome Web Store extensions steal user accounts, data
item.description: More than 100 malicious extensions in the official Chrome Web Store are attempting to steal Google OAuth2 Bearer tokens, deploy backdoors, and carry out ad fraud. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: EDR-Killer Ecosystem Expansion Requires Stronger BYOVD Defenses
item.description: Stopping EDR killers, which employ bring-your-own-vulnerable-driver (BYOVD) attack techniques, is difficult, but not impossible....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5082200 extended security update
item.description: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5082200 extended security update to fix the April 2026 Patch Tuesday vulnerabilities, including 2 zero-days. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: McGraw-Hill confirms data breach following extortion threat
item.description: Education company McGraw-Hill has confirmed in a statement to BleepingComputer that hackers exploited a Salesforce misconfiguration and accessed its internal data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
